feed.title: JunYian
feed.subtitle: Technical journal for hobbyist reverse engineering and machine learning exploration.

entry.title: Binary Golf Grand Prix 3
entry.summary: Introduction This is the quick write-up for my BGGP #3 submission. I’m keeping this rather brief compared to my usual write-ups as I’m in the middle of multiple activities at my day job which have been keeping me rather occupied. The challenge description and rules can be found at https://tmpout.sh/bggp/3/. Objectives The primary objective, is to find the smallest file that can crash a soft...

entry.title: Flare-On 2014 Challenge 2
entry.summary: Introduction Finally, the write-ups for all Challenge #1 across all Flare-on events to date are completed. As we move on to Challenge #2 series, the writeups will be less newbie-ish. This challenge consists of a HTML file home.html and an image flare-on.png. Environment Only a browser is sufficient. The newer Microsoft Edge is used here, which is based on Chromium. When opening the file hom...

entry.title: Flare-On 2021 Challenge 1
entry.summary: Introduction The challenge is called credchecker and consists of a HTML file admin.html with some images. This challenge is special to me as it’s the first official Flare-On challenge that I joined, and managed to complete. Environment Only a browser is sufficient. The newer Microsoft Edge is used here, which is based on Chromium. When opening the file admin.html, we’re greeted with the foll...

entry.title: Flare-On 2020 Challenge 1
entry.summary: Introduction The challenge file fidler.exe is a Python application. This challenge is rather unique as the Python source code is also provided with the challenge. The challenge message simply states that we should win the game by any means necessary to get the flag. Environment Of course, Python need to be installed. I installed Python 3.10.2 on Windows 11. The following image shows the fir...

entry.title: Flare-On 2019 Challenge 1
entry.summary: Introduction The challenge file MemeCatBattleStation.exe is a .NET application. This fact is stated in the challenge message, and even suggested to use dnSpy. But we’ll be using dnSpyEx instead to decompile the binary. Environment I just started trying Windows 11. Quite surprisingly, the challenge EXE didn’t prompt to install .NET framework. Reversing The initial window of the challenge ...
